steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side is an important new initiative, a noun phrase. The right side is the Timeline of African American Music, another noun phrase that names the specific initiative.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces a name, title, or elaboration. Option B correctly uses a colon—initiative: the Timeline of African American Music—to show that Timeline names the initiative.

The other options violate punctuation rules: two adjacent nouns without a connector (A), a period that fragments the sentence (C), and a semicolon that misuses rules for separating noun phrases (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
